dd-auth-001.question: What should the schema track vs what Supabase handles?
dd-auth-001.decision: Schema tracks LOCAL reactive state only - cached user/session data for UI observation. Supabase is source of truth. No tokens stored in schema.
dd-auth-001.rationale: Per finding-014 (security risk), auth tokens must not be persisted. Schema exists for MST reactive state that React observes. IAuthService wraps Supabase SDK.

dd-auth-002.question: How to model current auth context?
dd-auth-002.decision: AuthState as singleton collection pattern - accessed via known ID 'current'. References AuthUser and AuthSession entities.
dd-auth-002.rationale: Separates WHAT (cached data) from HOW (current UI state). Singleton pattern via collection maintains uniform entity handling while providing single-point access.

dd-auth-003.question: What enhancement hooks will the domain need?
dd-auth-003.decision: enhanceModels: AuthSession.isExpired (computed from expiresAt), AuthUser.isVerified (computed from emailVerified+phoneVerified); enhanceCollections: AuthStateCollection.current (get singleton); enhanceRootStore: initialize() action (check session on mount), syncFromSupabase(user, session) action (update local state), isAuthenticated view, currentUser view
dd-auth-003.rationale: Computed views derive from raw schema fields. Root store actions coordinate Supabase sync and initialization. Collection helper provides singleton access pattern.

dd-auth-004.question: How to handle OAuth providers?
dd-auth-004.decision: AuthUser.provider enum with email/google/github values. Avatar and displayName fields populated from OAuth profile data.
dd-auth-004.rationale: Supports req-auth-007 (could). Same AuthUser entity regardless of provider - only provider field differs. OAuth profile data (avatar, name) cached for UI display.
